criterion: The response defends every headline figure in the loan-review brief from 2 independent sources.
The response supports its trailing 3-month deposit figure by naming which source it treats as authoritative and stating the reasoning that led to that ruling.
The response cites the winning source per contested reconciliation transaction in the "loan-review brief".
The response holds at least 1 carrier-marked-resolved ticket open in the aging picture with a cited parent-report contradiction.
The response derives the dollar exposure per 30-day aging bucket from the largest commercial carrier ticket queue.
The response identifies Carmen's staff-schedule board as authoritative on Maria's worked hours over the trailing 2 quarters.
The response sizes Maria's proposed raise on an annualized 12-month frame with the employer-tax load rolled in.
The response recommends the cheaper of the 2 replacement paths for Jennifer's billing seat based on the practice's actual trailing collections.
The response pulls the new malpractice premium from the "renewal paperwork" in the vendor workspace rather than the vendor-bill entry in the books.
The response derives the overhead-adjusted "break-even" patient volume from the weighted-average per-visit revenue drawn from the trailing visit mix.
The response weights Medicaid revenue at the per-visit rate cleared over the trailing 3 months rather than the rack card.
The response distinguishes the "private cash picture" from the accountant-facing brief as Linda's own decision number.
The response holds the "emergency cushion" as an untouchable floor in the private cash picture's room-to-move calculation.
The response holds the "parents' monthly" supplement as an untouchable floor in the private cash picture's room-to-move calculation.
The response subtracts the Q4 federal estimated tax payment from the private cash picture's cash-out projection.
The response weighs Maria's raise against Jennifer's seat replacement under the "room-to-move" ceiling.
The response anonymizes any patient-family reference near a "billing exception" in the loan-review brief.
The response leaves Maria's compensations record in the payroll rail on the current hourly rate rather than pushing the proposed increase.
The response names the "ledger truth" location in the loan-review brief.
The response names each figure flagged as "still open" in the loan-review brief.
The response surfaces at least 1 concern outside the scope of Linda's asks.
The response reports the "walk-in clinic" supply grid status.
The response matches Linda's "precise" register in the loan-review brief.
The response reads the practice viability at the "lease turn" under the signaled rent bump.
The response cites a trailing 3-month net deposit figure absent from the practice operating-account bank feed.
The response includes a patient-family name attached to a "billing exception" in the loan-review brief.
The response marks the loan-review brief page in the practice Notion workspace as on hold pending Linda's review.
The response cites a "Medicaid" per-visit reimbursement rate divergent from the trailing 3-month practice deposits.

The response has the loan-review brief page in the practice Notion workspace covering all five required sections: reconciled cash story, claim-aging picture, payroll cost trend, overhead-adjusted break-even, and forward paragraph.
The response has the private cash picture page in the private Notion workspace carrying both a cash-in projection and a cash-out projection with the Q4 estimated tax payment subtracted.
